title: Multimodal Tokenization
content:
# Interview Prep: Multimodal

## Q1: How does CLIP's contrastive learning loss function optimize text-image similarity scales?

### Standard Answer
CLIP computes a similarity matrix $\mathbf{S} = \mathbf{I} \mathbf{T}^{\top}$ scaled by a learnable temperature $\tau$. Cross-entropy loss is applied along both columns and rows to maximize diagonal alignment (correct pairs) relative to all other indices in the batch.
